heading: The Hyrax Method
subheading: Five modules that mirror hyrax behavior: warming, scrambling, hauling, and bolting to cover. Mix 3 to 5 modules per session for a complete training day.

modules.name: Bask and Prime
modules.desc: Breathing, mobility, and isometrics to switch on without burning matches.

modules.alt: Athlete stepping and balancing on rocks
modules.name: Scramble
modules.desc: Climb, crawl, and footwork for confident movement on uneven terrain.

modules.name: Forage and Haul
modules.desc: Loaded carries and repeated pickups to build grip, trunk, and leg stamina.

modules.alt: Athlete holding a squat while scanning
modules.name: Sentinel
modules.desc: Freeze drills and reactive changes that train control under adrenaline.

modules.alt: Short sprint between cones on trail
modules.name: Bolt to Cover
modules.desc: 5 to 15 second bursts with recovery, repeated for high quality power.

splitBody: Hyrax Fitness avoids long steady blocks. It is built around repeatable micro efforts, efficient mechanics, and deliberate recovery.
bullets: Every session includes at least one carry and one scramble element.
Work happens in forage bouts of 20 to 90 seconds.
Breathing and composure are trained explicitly with Sentinel freezes.
Cooldown is mandatory: slow breathing and mobility to reset.
